title: Could Bruins be a viable landing spot for Darnell Nurse via trade?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Darnell Nurse has requested a trade from the Edmonton Oilers after 12 seasons, listing Boston as a potential destination.
The Boston Bruins need a top-four defenseman, with Nurse's $9.25 million annual cap hit posing a challenge to their $7.7 million cap space.
The Bruins might trade contracts and prospects like Mason Lohrei and Joonas Korposalo to acquire Nurse and bolster their defense.

title: Car carrying family of 3 pulled from Seekonk River in Rhode Island
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A car with a family of three was pulled from the Seekonk River near Taft Street, Rhode Island.
Rescue efforts faced challenging conditions due to strong currents and poor visibility, officials stated.
Authorities have not disclosed the identities or conditions of the vehicle's occupants as recovery efforts continue.

title: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes restaurant, shifts focus back to food truck
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Jamaica Mi Hungry closes its Downtown Boston restaurant, shifts focus to food truck and catering services.
The food truck will regularly serve Kendall Square, MIT, and Boston Common starting this summer.
The business, founded by Ernie Campbell, continues after closing its last brick-and-mortar in 2025.

title: Report finds former Canton Sgt. Sean Goode used ‘horrific’ language in texts
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Canton officials released an internal affairs report detailing offensive messages sent by former Sgt. Sean Goode.
Goode's messages, spanning over a decade, included racist, misogynistic, anti-Semitic, and other discriminatory content.
The Massachusetts POST Commission suspended Goode's certification, preventing him from working in law enforcement statewide.

title: Alvin Campbell Jr., serial rapist and AG’s brother, receives life in prison
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr., convicted of raping five women, was sentenced to life in prison in Boston.
Campbell, Attorney General Andrea Campbell's brother, was found guilty of 21 out of 22 charges.
Judge Mary K. Ames imposed additional sentences totaling over 100 years for Campbell's crimes.

title: Officials identify man killed in wrong-way crash in Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Riley Sergi, 28, from Haverhill was killed in a wrong-way crash on I-93 in Boston.
Antone Carvalho, 81, of Somerset, drove an SUV wrong-way on I-93, causing the fatal crash.
A family of four was hospitalized after their Honda Odyssey collided with an Audi A4 attempting to avoid Carvalho.

title: 5 takeaways from Paraguay’s upset win over Germany in World Cup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paraguay defeated Germany 1-1 (4-3) in penalty kicks at the World Cup, advancing to the Round of 16.
José Canale scored the winning penalty for Paraguay after a VAR review disallowed Germany's extra-time goal.
Paraguay fans celebrated their country's first knockout-round goal despite Germany's possession dominance throughout the match.

title: Revolution announce front office shift: Mass. native Chris Tierney named general manager
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Chris Tierney named general manager and chief soccer officer of New England Revolution.
Curt Onalfo promoted to senior vice president of soccer operations, focusing on team development.
Both Tierney and Onalfo will report to club president Brian Bilello.

title: Man allegedly pepper sprays woman jogging in apparent random assault in Maine coastal city
author: John Waller
bullets: A 37-year-old man allegedly pepper sprayed a woman jogging in Rockland, Maine, on Saturday.
The suspect was arrested and faces charges including assault and criminal use of disabling chemicals.
Witnesses reportedly chased the man away before police intervened in the apparent random assault.

title: Noah Kahan asks his audience to please not poop at venues (except in designated areas)
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Noah Kahan urges fans to use designated bathrooms after an incident during his Philadelphia concert.
A Reddit post describes a concertgoer defecating in their seat to avoid missing songs.
Kahan also addresses fans stealing street signs in his hometown, offering to cover replacement costs.
